title: hydrangea scandens var. cordifolia
intro: Hydrangea scandens var. cordifolia, also known as the Heartleaf Hydrangea, is a climbing shrub native to North America, characterized by its large, ovate leaves and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The leaves of the Heartleaf Hydrangea have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system.
